title: Add an API for Attach over HTTP API
body:
The new APIv2 branch provides an HTTP-based remote API to Podman. The requirements of this are, unfortunately, incompatible with the existing Attach API. For non-terminal attach, we need append a header to what was copied from the container, to multiplex STDOUT and STDERR; to do this with the old API, we'd need to copy into an intermediate buffer first, to handle the headers.

To avoid this, provide a new API to handle all aspects of terminal and non-terminal attach, including closing the hijacked HTTP connection. This might be a bit too specific, but for now, it seems to be the simplest approach.

This commit provides the base API only; APIv2 has not merged yet, so this is not wired into the frontend.

I wanted to rework the rest of attach at the same time (some parts of it, particularly how we start the attach session and how we do resizing, are (in my opinion) handled much better here. That may still be on the table, but I wanted to avoid breaking existing APIs in this already massive change.

comments.body: Marking WIP until APIv2 merges and I can integrate this into it. This will supercede #4767

Terminal attach is working. Removing WIP.

Non-terminal attach still needs to be tested.
Actually, this still needs significant fixes, now that I look at it, so don't merge yet

Non-terminal attach confirmed working.

Lingering issues:
- Need to write errors to the HTTP connection
- Hangup on container exit is still iffy - seems to hang for a few seconds figuring out what happened
- Need to make sure we are in a good state to attach before hijacking
Issues 1 and 2 are addressed. Just needs swagger docs and proper state checks.
Apparently there are query parameters. Will deal with those and swagger docs tomorrow.
@rhatdan @vrothberg @baude @jwhonce This should be ready now, PTAL
Pre-review comment: Can you squash the commits? I am mostly concerned about noise when bisecting.
